title: pteretis pensylvanica f. foliacea
intro: Pteretis pensylvanica f. foliacea, also known as Western yarrow, is a subspecies of the yarrow plant that is native to Western North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Western yarrow include reducing fever, relieving menstrual cramps, and soothing digestive issues. Western yarrow can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in herbal remedies such as salves and poultices to aid in the treatment of various health conditions.
